frameworks.name: multi-dimensional population coding
frameworks.one_line: PFC encodes decision variables across multiple neural activity dimensions

frameworks.name: one-dimensional attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: single-attractor account of decision-making the paper argues against

frameworks.name: rotational dynamics
frameworks.one_line: late-phase rotational regime extends linear accumulator picture

frameworks.name: context-dependent perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: task setting in which PFC selects relevant sensory evidence

core_question: How does monkey prefrontal cortex population activity encode decisions, context, and sensory evidence during context-dependent perceptual choice?
key_assumptions: task-relevant decision variables are linearly readable from PFC population activity
neural code can be decomposed via identifiable task-relevant dimensions
single PFC population can carry multiple task variables simultaneously
manipulations: task context (which sensory feature is relevant vs irrelevant)
time within the stimulus integration period
key_findings: PFC carries a multi-dimensional code for decision, context, and both relevant and irrelevant sensory information
representations evolve from an early linear accumulation phase to a later rotational-dynamics phase
the two phases arise from one broadly tuned population, not separate subpopulations
transition to rotational dynamics coincides with a sustained plateau in decoding accuracy
interpretations: PFC decision coding is multi-dimensional rather than a one-dimensional attractor
rotational dynamics preserve sensory and choice information throughout stimulus integration
